free_text: Interaction of vanadium with protons of energies up
to 84 MeV
Experiment performed using the
internal beam of the McGill synchrocyclotron.
The target materials were vanadium foils of normal
isotopic composition with thickness of 0.002 in.
Cooper foils with thickness of 0.002 in. were added
for monitoring purposes.Initially three foils of
copper and two of vanadium were used in the foil stack.
In the later bombardments, one foil of copper and one
of vanadium were used as targets for bombarding
energies up to 30 Mev
NaI(Tl) detector was used to detect decay
gamma rays
Activation method with radiochemical
separation of reaction products was used. Different
separation methods for different radionuclides were
used.
for EN < 16MeV
for EN > 16MeV
Meadows monitor
cross section data, as corrected by Meghir
Ph.D. Thesis, McGill University, Montreal, Que. 1962,
were used.
Compiled by S.H.
